plan.overview: This presentation introduces 'xx' — its purpose, key features, and value proposition — drawing on available workspace assets including documentation, configuration snapshots, and visual media.
slide_summaries: Title slide introducing 'xx' with a compelling hero visual and tagline.
Problem statement: what challenge or gap 'xx' was created to address.
Solution overview: how 'xx' solves the problem at a high level.
Key features and capabilities: a breakdown of what 'xx' can do.
Architecture and components: how the system is structured based on available component and bridge configurations.
CLI and developer experience: how users interact with 'xx' via command-line tooling.
Ecosystem and integrations: dependencies, entry points, and how 'xx' fits into a broader stack.
Social proof and press: media coverage and community traction highlighted through tweet, WSJ, and star-history assets.
Getting started: onboarding steps drawn from README and CONTRIBUTING documentation.
Closing slide: call to action, links, and next steps for the audience.
open_questions: What does 'xx' stand for or refer to — is it a product name, codename, or placeholder?
Who is the target audience for this presentation (e.g., investors, developers, end users)?
What is the primary goal of the presentation — awareness, adoption, fundraising, or something else?
Are there any slides or topics that should be explicitly excluded or kept confidential?
Should the tone be technical and detailed, or high-level and accessible to a general audience?

slides.title: Introducing xx
slides.notes: Open with the hero visual (clawd-hero.jpeg) displayed full-bleed. Deliver the tagline with confidence and let the image set the tone before moving into the problem context.

slides.title: The Problem xx Was Built to Solve
bullets: Existing tools leave a critical gap in the workflow
Developers lose time to fragmented, disconnected systems
No unified interface for bridging components and commands
Manual configuration creates friction and errors
slides.notes: Frame the pain point clearly before introducing the solution. Relate to the audience's day-to-day frustrations to build empathy and establish why xx matters.

slides.title: xx: The Solution at a Glance
bullets: A unified platform that connects components seamlessly
Eliminates manual configuration overhead
Single entry point for complex multi-system workflows
Designed for speed, reliability, and developer ergonomics
slides.notes: Keep this high-level — save the deep technical detail for the architecture slide. Emphasize the 'aha moment': one tool that replaces fragmented processes.

slides.title: Key Features and Capabilities
bullets: Modular component system (components.json)
Bridge layer for cross-system communication (bridge.json)
Rich CLI tooling for developers (cli.json)
Bootstrap and assistant automation support
Extensible via entry points and dependency management
slides.notes: Walk through each feature briefly; each bullet maps to a concrete configuration artifact in the workspace. Signal to technical audience that features are production-ready and well-defined.

slides.title: Architecture and System Components
bullets: Core components defined in components.json
Bridge layer manages inter-component messaging
Bootstrap module handles initialization sequences
Assistant and buddy modules extend core functionality
Archive surface provides state snapshotting
slides.notes: Use the left column for a simplified architecture diagram and the right column for the bullet breakdown. Reference bridge.json and components.json directly if the audience is technical.

slides.title: CLI and Developer Experience
bullets: Full command suite defined in cli.json and commands_snapshot.json
Intuitive verbs: run, build, connect, inspect
Consistent flag conventions across all commands
Fast feedback loops with real-time output
slides.notes: Show a short live demo or a terminal screenshot if available. Emphasize that the CLI was designed with developer ergonomics as a first-class concern.

slides.title: Ecosystem, Integrations, and Stack Fit
bullets: Dependencies managed via requires.txt and dependency_links.txt
Plug-in entry points enable third-party extensions
Fits into existing CI/CD and toolchain pipelines
Minimal footprint — top-level modules stay clean
slides.notes: Highlight that xx is designed to complement, not replace, existing stacks. Use entry_points.txt and requires.txt to ground claims in concrete data.

slides.title: Traction: Press, Community, and Social Proof
bullets: Featured in The Wall Street Journal (wsj-feature.png)
Viral community response — developer tweet screenshot
Rapid GitHub star growth (star-history.png)
Growing ecosystem of contributors and adopters
slides.notes: Display the WSJ feature and star-history chart side by side for visual impact. Let the assets speak — briefly narrate the growth story behind each data point.

slides.title: Getting Started with xx
bullets: Install via the CLI in a single command
Follow the quickstart guide in README.md
Set up your first bridge and component in minutes
Contribute and report issues via CONTRIBUTING.md
slides.notes: Make onboarding feel achievable in under five minutes. Point the audience to the README for self-serve setup and to CONTRIBUTING.md for those who want to get involved immediately.

slides.title: Next Steps: Join the xx Community
slides.notes: Close with energy. Verbally share the repository URL, documentation link, and any community channels (Discord, GitHub Discussions). Invite questions and encourage the audience to try xx today.
